paragraphs.body: In the process of creating non-AI-assisted and AI-assisted videos, I experienced two different creative approaches. The traditional method, like Video 1, required me to conceive every detail from scratch, which gave me complete creative freedom. By creating different scenes, it was expressed with better effects, but it also brought more challenges and time investment. In contrast, when using AI Vyond Go, I felt an increase in efficiency, but I didn't find it to be a particularly good experience.

For the AI-assisted video, I used prompts similar to "the development and changes in Pittsburgh's Hill District and Shanghai's old town 50 years from now." Choosing more specific prompts was intended to explore how AI handles cross-cultural and cross-temporal urban development themes. Our group's goal was to showcase potential trends and challenges in future urban development by comparing two areas with rich historical backgrounds, while also prompting viewers to think about urban planning, cultural preservation, and sustainable development.

AI Vyond has advantages in video production, but its limitations cannot be ignored. Its main advantage is quickly generating basic scenes and providing initial creative frameworks. However, its limitations were more apparent than expected. The AI-generated scenes tend to be monotonous, lacking richness and diversity. Moreover, when dealing with future themes, AI seems to struggle with autonomously incorporating sufficient futuristic elements. These limitations led me to make numerous adjustments and additions in post-production. I had to add more futuristic elements myself, such as robots and more futuristic offices, to enhance the required sense of the future in the video.

Nevertheless, I still strongly believe that AI can serve as a starting point and source of inspiration for the creative process. It provides a basic framework that allows me to engage in deeper creation and personalization. This process made me realize that effectively utilizing AI tools requires me to be more diligent in video production to compensate for AI's shortcomings in detail and creative depth.

I was not very satisfied with the initial video generated by Vyond Go. Compared to my expectations, the AI-generated content had obvious deficiencies. The video failed to accurately present future urban landscapes and technological changes. Furthermore, AI's performance in reflecting the unique cultural characteristics and historical continuity of Pittsburgh's Hill District and Shanghai's old town was far from adequate. The generated scenes were too general and generic, completely lacking the local characteristics of these two areas. The data and information provided in the AI's background narration lacked reliability and accuracy. These data could not be verified.

To improve the AI-generated video, my group made a series of modifications. We added more iconic buildings and cultural elements that reflect regional characteristics, like our school’s icon and the Cathedral of Learning. We also created scenes that better highlight the future and contain more information. In the end, we adjusted the character roles to better represent different cultural backgrounds, like a Chinese and an American figure. Through this experiment, I realized that AI video production tools have limitations in handling complex, cross-cultural video production themes.
